Therapy-induced remodeling of IgG and plasma N-glycans in breast cancer

evidence_entities.text: Chemotherapy induced significant decrease in IgG core fucosylation
anthracycline-based regimen led to decreased core fucosylation and digalactosylation and increased monogalactosylation
The greatest reduction in core fucosylation was observed in patients treated with docetaxel and cyclophosphamide
HER2-targeted therapy was associated with decreased bisecting N-acetylglucosamine
oligomannose glycans increased in patients following chemotherapy
Tumor size was significantly associated with several plasma glycan traits, particularly digalactosylation and oligomannosylation
